executive_summary: No new analyst action required. The PRED-105 silent-ignore is now ~3.92 days past closure (6 consecutive polls; predictions.html byte-identical for 5 polls; last manual author commit a57e731 unchanged for 7 polls / ~106h). The pre-locked POST-CLOSURE RUBRIC option (iii) was executed by the decider on 2026-04-20 (FAIL-009 added). Prior run identified hallucinated text in FAIL-009 and proposed a parallel FAIL-010 for PRED-072; both have since been actioned (FAIL-009 now correctly describes the 46° copper-coil cucumber-growth experiment; FAIL-010 exists for PRED-072 TTB diurnal). The current poll therefore confirms the pattern continues without escalation. None of the three pre-locked next-poll escalation triggers fired: (1) no goalpost-move on the 14-day window text, (2) no WIN-070 added with a PRED-105 claim, (3) no first manual commit since a57e731. Canary opentimestamps-provenance remains SOFT-TRIGGERED with no structural fix in evidence (no predictions_only.json.ots, no monitor.py separation of predictions from audits, no NOAA/USGS references in timestamping logic, no provenance-keyword commit messages).

kernel_of_truth.pipeline_health: The change-detection pipeline correctly continues to flag PRED-105 as substantive/HIGH every poll even though no new dome-side action has occurred. That is the correct behavior — silence past a falsifiable test window IS a substantive finding, and the rising elapsed-time counter (52h → 62.5h → 70.2h → 82.1h → 94.1h) is itself the evidence we need for the silent-drop pattern. Crediting the poller for not lowering priority just because a continuation looks routine.
kernel_of_truth.charitable_dome_reading: A 14-day cucumber-growth experiment in a home lab can plausibly require a few extra days for write-up — the falsification criterion (≥15% growth-rate delta) needs measurements, photographs, and a published methodology. 3-5 days post-window is inside what an institutional preprint workflow can take. The predictions.html being byte-identical for 5 polls is consistent with the author simply not having posted the result yet rather than active concealment.
kernel_of_truth.why_kernel_does_not_save_the_claim: The 14-day test window already budgets the full growth interval; the closure date IS the date by which the experiment should be complete and the result posted. 94+ hours past closure with byte-identical site state and zero manual author commits across 7 polls (~4.4 days) is no longer plausibly within a write-up delay — it is silent slippage past a deadline the author chose. The pre-registered SHA-256 anchor is still pinned to the original wording, so the silence is recorded against the original protocol; the dome cannot retroactively claim the prediction was never made. The rubric we locked on 2026-04-17 was designed for exactly this case: silent slippage is a defensible adverse outcome we can record against the dome.

Crypto hash 3c427a83edaeaf33c884264dedffbedece14378fc766bdffeeac0b4d4d517490 unchanged. No PASS/FAIL/REFINED label. Window text still reads literal "April 18, 2026 (14-day window)" — no goalpost-move.
chg_20260421_2207_001.verdict_this_run: No action. This is the 6th consecutive continuation poll of a silent-ignore pattern that was actioned by the decider on 2026-04-20 (FAIL-009). The 4-day threshold the change record references is not a locked rubric trigger — the analyst rubric used 48–72h to decide the entry-add, which has long since fired. No further bookkeeping action is triggered by mere elapsed-time growth.

additional_watch_added_this_run.signal: PRED-105 silence reaches 168h+ (one week) past closure with no dome action
additional_watch_added_this_run.action_if_fired: Consider promoting our_verdict in data/predictions.json from pending to a post-silence terminal category. This requires a design decision (separate analyst run / EXP) about how to render post-silence verdicts in Section 11. Do not act unilaterally.
additional_watch_added_this_run.rationale: A week is long enough that even charitable home-lab write-up timelines no longer apply. Documenting this trigger now so we have it queued before it fires.

counter_considerations.consideration: Should we update FAIL-009 summary text from "52+ hours later" to reflect current ~94h elapsed?
counter_considerations.take: No. The summary captures the moment-of-add as a snapshot; updating on every poll would create noise commits and lose the original-add provenance. The dome_ref_current field could be refreshed to "PENDING-PROSPECTIVE (94h past window)" but that too is best done in a single update when we eventually adjudicate (e.g. promote our_verdict). Holding for now.

counter_considerations.consideration: Has the silent-ignore now persisted long enough to be self-evidently a falsification rather than a delay?
counter_considerations.take: For our review, the FAIL entry is already booked. The deeper question — should our_verdict in predictions.json be promoted from pending to silent_drop_falsified — is a distinct design call. I am opening a watch trigger at the one-week mark (168h, ~2026-04-25) so this gets a deliberate analyst look rather than drifting into routine. Charitable view: the registered SHA-256 still anchors the original protocol so we can adjudicate at any time without losing temporal priority. Less charitable: continuing to hold the per-prediction verdict at pending while the FAIL entry exists could be read as us being soft on the dome — but that is a presentation problem we can address with a Section 11 rendering convention rather than a per-prediction verdict change.

counter_considerations.consideration: Did the 4-day threshold mentioned in the change record match a locked rubric we should be acting on?
counter_considerations.take: No. The locked rubric (analyst 2026-04-17) used 48–72h as the silent-ignore decision band for the FAIL-entry add, which fired at 52h on 2026-04-20. There is no second locked threshold at 4 days. The poller mentioning the 4-day mark is informational, not a trigger. If we want a 1-week trigger (which I am proposing above), that should be deliberately added rather than ad-hoc.

counter_considerations.consideration: Does the operator-authored CHANGE-decider-pop-timestamp-hoist record warrant any analyst comment on review-state.json?
counter_considerations.take: No. It is a pipeline-internal bugfix with explicit non_impact field stating no semantic change to any dome-site claim. The fact that it landed in monitor/changes/ rather than monitor/integrity/ is mildly unusual but not an analyst concern — the decision of where to file pipeline-meta records belongs to tinker/operator. Logged for completeness only.

overall_threat_level: LOW. Continuation poll. No new dome behavior, no canary motion, no external problem reports. The silent-ignore is now well-documented and tracked; further bookkeeping triggers fire only on (a) goalpost-move, (b) WIN-070 announcement, (c) first manual commit, or (d) the proposed one-week silence threshold. Previous analyst recommendations have all been actioned by the decider.
gotchas: Do NOT touch FAIL-009 or FAIL-010. Their text is correct and their snapshot timestamps are part of the audit trail.

Do NOT update data/predictions.json PRED-105 our_verdict in this run. Promotion from pending to a post-silence terminal category requires a design decision (Section 11 rendering convention) that has not been made.
Do NOT trigger breaking-news on this continuation. Newsworthy preconditions (goalpost, WIN-070, OTS structural fix) are not met.
Do NOT clear or change canary status. opentimestamps-provenance remains SOFT-TRIGGERED until the author separates prediction documents from observation documents and timestamps them independently.

Do NOT escalate the 4-day threshold mentioned in the change record into action. It is informational; the locked rubric fired at 48–72h and has executed. The proposed 1-week trigger I am adding to next_poll_escalation_watch is for future consideration, not immediate action.
tldr: PRED-105 silent-ignore continues at ~94h (~3.92 days) past closure, 6th consecutive poll. FAIL-009 (correct cucumber-growth text) and FAIL-010 (PRED-072 parallel) are both already in place from prior runs. No new dome action, no escalation triggers fired, canary unchanged. No analyst action this run beyond logging the continuation and adding a 1-week silence watch trigger for future consideration.
